name: Lime-Pine Marmalade
recipeInstructions:
Makes about 7 half-pints. Dice or put the lime and grapefruit through a meat grinder after washing and removing seeds. Chop the pineapple. Measure all fruit including juice, adding 1½ cups water per cup of fruit; let stand overnight. Simmer uncovered over low heat until fruit is tender (about 1 hour). Measure again, add orange rind and sugar (1 cup per cup of pulp) then cook over medium heat until sugar dissolves. Stir constantly. Cook over high heat until thermometer reads 220-222°F or syrup sheets form when 2 drops fall from a spoon. Pour into hot sterilized jars, sealing immediately.
To seal: Fill jars leaving ½-inch head space, wiping rims and threads with damp cloth. Cover with melted paraffin (1/8 inch), allowing it to set before adding another layer to seal completely.
